symbols: communal dance — the joy of human fellowship and celebration rooted in cultural tradition
mountain backdrop — the Aragonese Pyrenees framing the regional identity of the dancers
traditional costumes — the preservation of cultural heritage and communal identity
circle of dancers — community, unity, and shared participation in festive life
bright sunlight — the vitality and warmth of the celebration
description: A vibrant outdoor scene depicting a large group of men and women performing a traditional Aragonese folk dance (jota) in a mountainous landscape. The dancers wear colorful regional costumes: women in full dark skirts with bright shawls and headscarves, men in dark vests and white shirts. Several figures are captured mid-movement with arms raised. The group forms a loose circle on open ground before dramatic brown and green mountains that fill the background under a bright sky.
